confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Score of 4 reflects high but not maximum corrigibility: the Section 1 proportionality mechanism is among the strongest constitutionally embedded self-correction systems in the dataset, but the notwithstanding clause (s.33) provides a legislative escape valve that has been used, capping the score below maximum.

reviewNote: Score value 4/5 defensible: constitutional-containment mechanism (Canadian Charter s.1/s.2) represents high corrigibility with one caveat — the notwithstanding clause (s.33) creates a legislative override that is the documented uncertainty factor. Confidence 0.65 reflects this structural complication. Ready for human-reviewed pending chain expansion to ≥4 passages.
